(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke up in Issaquah with that early-June sun already spilling through the blinds, coffee on, and my little setup list open like it’s a prayer. I grabbed a bagel and cream cheese to-go from Bagel Delite, filled my thermos, and headed out while the day still felt quiet. The roads were steady on the way to Bothell, and I spent the morning at Bothell Ceramics Studio working through glazing—mugs, small bowls, all those tiny decisions about thickness and shine that never stop feeling personal. Lunch at Crown Hill Cafe hit the spot, then I swung by REI for packaging supplies and a couple craft-supply odds and ends. Later I drove out toward Tiger Mountain State Forest, took a short walk, and hunted for mushrooms like I always do, jotting damp patches in my foraging log. By the evening, downtown Seattle was lively—caught USA vs Wales at an early-opening bar near Seattle Center. I’m tired, but good-tired, like everything lined up.
thought_process: I planned the day around keeping my production steady and not getting stranded by transit quirks—especially since the light rail shut between Westlake and the University District. That closure nudged me toward driving and keeping errands tight, so I could still pick up packaging at Totem Lake without losing time. At the studio I stuck with glazing and finishing because those are “moment-sensitive” tasks: once the timing’s off, the batch is basically a write-off. Lunch at Crown Hill Cafe felt like the practical reset I needed before the REI run. The match airing at 11am PDT made my evening feel lighter, and the downtown buzz carried me through the commute. My mood shifted from focused-in-the-morning to relaxed-by-night, like the city’s energy matched my own pace.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
